title: Swarm-style dynamic handoffs between specialist Morty roles
source: OpenAI Swarm / handoff routing pattern & LangGraph Command-based handoffs (from prior knowledge; live fetch blocked)
description: Swarm-style orchestration lets an agent hand off control to a more appropriate specialist mid-task instead of the orchestrator pre-assigning one role for the whole ticket. Today a ticket is delegated to a single Morty role for its lifetime; if the work turns out to need a different specialty (e.g. a backend ticket that needs a security pass), there's no clean way to route it without orchestrator intervention. Letting a worker emit a structured handoff request enables adaptive routing and better role-to-work matching.
proposed_changes: Extend the MortyHandoff schema (schemas.py) with an optional `handoff_to: {role: str, reason: str}` field. In persona.py define the available specialist roles and their trigger conditions in the worker system prompt. In orchestrate.py, when a handoff object contains handoff_to, re-delegate the ticket to that role with the prior worker's summary as context (guard against loops with a visited-roles set and a max-hops limit). Emit a roro 'handoff' event so the terminal renders the role transition.
